beats.s_morning: race morning in {TOWN}: bunting on every bridge and the mill river full of glitter; ten-year-old {HERO} carries {COMPANION}, a small pine boat with the leaf-shaped sail her friend {FRIEND} painted last summer, before the quarrel neither of them will mention; every racer carries a long-handled bank net, and the rules are painted on the boathouse door: nets only to free a stuck boat, carrying only at the white portage posts, every boat home by the {DEADLINE}, and racers stay on the bank paths, always
beats.s_launch: at the {ENTRANCE} {OFFICIAL} drops the checkered flag and a hundred small boats tumble into the current while the bank path fills with running kids; {RIVAL} falls in step beside {HERO}, her sleek red {RIVAL_BOAT} already nosing ahead of {COMPANION}; somewhere back in the crowd {FRIEND} runs alone behind the patched boat {FRIEND_BOAT}; ahead the river bends toward {SPLIT_LANDMARK}, where the water divides
beats.s_split: at {SPLIT_LANDMARK} the river splits three ways and {COMPANION} spins in the choosing eddy; to the left the {ROUTE_A_NAME} runs {ROUTE_A_LURE}; ahead the {ROUTE_B_NAME} loops {ROUTE_B_LURE} through the {B1_HERON}; to the right the {ROUTE_C_NAME} slides {ROUTE_C_LURE} toward the clanking {C_GATES}; the current is about to take her, and {HERO} must pick one bank path and commit to wherever it leads
beats.a_dash: the {ROUTE_A_NAME} path: {ROUTE_A_CHAR}; {COMPANION} shoots ahead like she was built for this channel, and {RIVAL} pounds along at {HERO}'s shoulder, {RIVAL_BOAT} and {COMPANION} trading the lead stroke for stroke; then, ahead, {A_WILLOW} lies right across the water
beats.a_snag: the fallen {A_WILLOW}: last winter's storm dropped a whole tree into the race, and its trailing branches have combed up half the fleet, boats pinned like beads on a string; {FRIEND} is there hauling at a branch, {FRIEND_BOAT} stuck deep in the tangle; {COMPANION} is sliding fast toward the one open gap under the trunk, and {RIVAL} is already running on past the snag
beats.a_gap: {COMPANION} threads the {A_WILLOW} gap with a scrape of twigs and pops out clean; now it is just {HERO} and {RIVAL} at the front of the race, two boats and two runners with the towpath drumming underfoot, and the thunder of the millwheel growing ahead
beats.a_neck: neck and neck along the old race wall, {RIVAL_BOAT} leading by a bowsprit, then {COMPANION}, then {RIVAL_BOAT} again; spray hangs in the air like glitter; ahead the channel forks around the {A_ISLAND}, and there is no seeing both sides of the island at once
beats.a_wheel: the channel splits around the {A_ISLAND}: on the wheel side it dives under the dripping {A1_WHEEL_ARCH} beside the great turning wheel, wild water and rainbow spray; on the wall side it runs smooth and glassy past the miller's window toward the finish {A1_WALL_CHUTE}; whichever bank path {HERO} takes, she loses sight of the other, and both boats are drifting toward the split
beats.a_arch: under the {A1_WHEEL_ARCH} the world is roar and rainbow; {HERO} runs the railed parapet walk high above the channel, spray rattling on her hood, while below her {COMPANION} and {RIVAL_BOAT} buck through the standing waves side by side and shoot toward the {A1_WHEEL_ZONE} beneath the great turning wheel
beats.a_swirl: the {A1_WHEEL_ZONE} catches both boats in its slow merry-go-round: {RIVAL_BOAT} circles the wide outer ring, {COMPANION} the tight inner one, and neither can break loose; from the stone parapet a racer may reach down with a net to free a stuck boat, but there is time for only one good reach before the rings carry them past; {RIVAL} arrives gasping, her own net left far back at the {A_WILLOW}
beats.a_free_rocket: {HERO} kneels at the parapet rail and tips {RIVAL_BOAT} loose with one clean lift of the net; {RIVAL} whoops and sprints for the finish path; a heartbeat later the {A1_WHEEL_ZONE}, as if it approved of the trade, spits {COMPANION} out of the inner ring all on its own, one boat-length behind
beats.a_grace_end: {RIVAL_BOAT} takes the ribbon and {COMPANION} crosses second, and somehow second feels enormous; {RIVAL} splits the winner's {TREAT} straight down the middle on the judges' table and tells everyone the parapet reach saved her race; a rival at the start line, a friend at the finish
beats.a_wait_ring: {HERO} waits, net low, counting the inner ring like a skipping rope; on the third pass {COMPANION}'s bow swings under the parapet, and one gentle lift breaks her free of the merry-go-round while {RIVAL_BOAT} is still circling the outer rim
beats.a_outflow: {COMPANION} shoots the outflow first and takes the last straight with the current behind her; a moment later the pool lets {RIVAL_BOAT} go too, one boat-length back, and both runners fly along the bank toward the finish {A1_WALL_CHUTE}, shouting themselves hoarse
beats.a_ribbon_end: {COMPANION} noses over the line a hand's width ahead of {RIVAL_BOAT} and the bank explodes; the river ribbon is blue with a little tin boat sewn to it, and when {OFFICIAL} pins it on, {RIVAL} shakes {HERO}'s hand hard and promises that next year she is watching that inner ring; the leaf sail flutters like it knows
beats.a_wall: the wall side runs smooth as poured glass; through the mill's open window flour-dusty {A_MILLER} waves a white hand, and {RIVAL}'s shout fades beyond the island, committed to the wheel side; then, just before the finish {A1_WALL_CHUTE}, trouble: two lead boats locked mast to mast, spinning slow circles in the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth
beats.a_tangle: the tangled pair belongs to two big kids already at the {FINISH}, yelling useless advice from the rope line; the spinning boats block the middle of the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} and {COMPANION} is closing fast; from the plank bridge over the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth a net could tease the crossed masts apart, or {HERO} could trust {COMPANION}'s luck through the thin gap along the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} wall and save her sprint for the line
beats.a_untangle_pair: kneeling on the plank bridge, {HERO} works the net head between the crossed masts, one twist, two, and the boats spring apart like startled ducks and wobble off down the {A1_WALL_CHUTE}; the big kids' cheer from the rope line rattles the handrail
beats.a_chute_clear: the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} runs clear; the freed pair ride through first and second, and {COMPANION} sweeps in behind them, third and closing, the {FINISH} opening ahead full of flags and noise and the judge's raft gleaming at the line
beats.a_shoulder_offer: the big kids come pounding back along the rope line, patting their shoulders: hop up, best view of the finish in {TOWN}, we owe you a ride; it is a glorious offer, but behind {HERO} the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth has no keeper now, and more tired boats are wobbling toward it in twos and threes
beats.a_chute_guard: {HERO} plants herself on the plank bridge like a {C_LOCK} keeper; twice more the net flicks and twice more a tangle becomes a finish; below her, {COMPANION} holds third across the line while her skipper is busy holding the door open for everyone else
beats.a_clean_chute_end: third place is a yellow ribbon, but what the {FINISH} chants is the word keeper; every boat that crossed clean came through {HERO}'s {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth, and the big kids hoist her up anyway, ribbon and net and all, for a shoulder ride she did not have to trade the race for
beats.a_shoulder_ride: up on the big kids' shoulders the whole finish spreads out like a painted map: flags, the judge's raft, the last bend gleaming; it is the best view in {TOWN}, which is exactly how {HERO} sees the new tangle forming in the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth below, with a small familiar leaf sail drifting straight for it
beats.a_shoulders_end: by the time {HERO} reaches the plank bridge, {COMPANION} is wedged nose-first in the new tangle, and a marshal frees the knot only after the leaders are home; ninth place, and a lesson with a view: a keeper who leaves the gate learns the gate was hers; the big kids, mortified, promise shoulder rides all summer
beats.a_luck_gap: {COMPANION} feints at the thin gap along the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} wall, kisses one spinning hull with a tap like a knuckle on wood, tips until her leaf sail brushes the water, and somehow rolls back up; the gap opens, closes, opens, and she is through the spinners with the line in sight
beats.a_spinout_end: luck carries {COMPANION} past the spinners and then spends itself: she skates wide off the last eddy and noses soft into the reed fringe a boat-length short of the line; by the time the net frees her the ribbons are pinned; twelfth, upright and unscratched, while the tangled pair never finished at all; next year, {HERO} thinks, she will stop and untangle
beats.a_stop: {HERO} skids to a stop under the {A_WILLOW}, and behind her the choosing eddy folds {COMPANION} gently back into the {A_WILLOW}'s keep with the rest of the caught fleet; up close the snag is a curtain of trailing branches with six small boats pinned behind it, {FRIEND_BOAT} deepest of all; {FRIEND} hauls one-handed at a limb, not looking up, and the silence between them is as tangled as the boats
beats.a_helpers: there are two ways to open the {A_WILLOW}: the big limb pinning {FRIEND_BOAT} would lift if two people heaved together, which means standing next to {FRIEND} and finding something to say; or the whole trailing curtain could be levered up at once from the footbridge with a long net pole, freeing every boat at a stroke, if one runner is strong and quick enough alone
beats.a_big_branch: {HERO} sets her hands beside {FRIEND}'s on the wet bark, and the old count from last summer comes out of her mouth on its own, one two HUP; {FRIEND}'s grin arrives before the memory of the quarrel can stop it; the limb lifts, the curtain sways, and six boats slide free in a squadron, {FRIEND_BOAT} and {COMPANION} side by side
beats.a_crew_ask: the race is far downstream now, its noise a rumor; {FRIEND} wrings out a sleeve and nods at the two boats below the bank: crew up, my legs and your net, we could still catch the tail of the fleet; but {FRIEND_BOAT} is riding low, a thin {A2_BRANCH_SEAM} weeping river into her hull, and {FRIEND} is pretending very hard not to see it
beats.a_chase: a crew of two works like one racer: {FRIEND} reads the current lines and calls them, {HERO} runs the net, and the towpath flies; boat by boat the tail of the fleet comes back to them, {FRIEND_BOAT} gamely thumping along in {COMPANION}'s wake
beats.a_merge_pass: at the meadow merge, where all three channels braid back into one river, the two boats slip past a knot of tired stragglers in a single clean move, {FRIEND} calling the seam of fast water, {HERO} lifting one grounded boat clear of their lane; the finish flags show over the hedge
beats.a_crew_end: {FRIEND_BOAT} and {COMPANION} cross the line mid-fleet and exactly together, too far back for ribbons and too pleased to care; somewhere along the last field the quarrel simply is not there anymore, worked loose like the {A_WILLOW} limb; crew of two, says {FRIEND}, spitting on a palm and holding it out, same time next year
beats.a_caulk: {HERO} points at the weeping {A2_BRANCH_SEAM} and says it out loud, and it is easier than it looked; they beach {FRIEND_BOAT} on the grass, bail her dry with cupped hands, and pack the seam with moss the way {FRIEND}'s grandpa taught them both in the good summer, working like one pair of hands with four thumbs
beats.a_second_launch: {FRIEND_BOAT} relaunches tight and dry as a drum; the river is empty now, the race long gone, and the two of them run the whole mill reach anyway with both boats abreast, laughing at nothing, calling turns nobody is racing them for
beats.a_after_bell_end: the {AWARD_TABLE} is being folded up when two boats and two racers come up the last field together; logged home safe, listed with no place; and still it is somehow the best run either of them has ever had on this river, which is the sort of prize the {AWARD_TABLE} has no drawer for; same time next year, both of them, first ones at the flag
beats.a_bridge: {HERO} takes the footbridge steps two at a time, hooks her net pole under the {A_WILLOW}'s trailing curtain, and heaves; the whole green mass rises like a stage curtain going up, water streaming off it in silver strings, and the pinned fleet stares up at daylight
beats.a_curtain_lift: a roar from the bank as six boats pour through the opened gap in a body, {FRIEND_BOAT} among them and {COMPANION} swept along in the crowd; {FRIEND} waves both arms from the path; then the {A_WILLOW} takes its revenge: the curtain sags, and the net pole is swallowed to the handle, stuck fast
beats.a_pole_stuck: the pole will not come, the fleet is away downstream with nobody watching {COMPANION}, and on the far rail of the footbridge stands {A_MILLER} from the mill with a {A2_BRIDGE_SPARE}, holding it out: trade you, mine for the story of how you lifted the whole {A_WILLOW}, told to my mum at the mill door; the big pole might still be wrestled free, but wrestling takes time
beats.a_wrestle: {HERO} braces both boots against the bridge rail post and hauls until the {A_WILLOW} gives up the pole with a sound like a cork; it costs a hundred heartbeats and both shoulders, but the long net is hers again as she pelts down the towpath after the vanished fleet
beats.a_pole_free: she catches the race at the {A_ISLAND}, passing knots of parents who turn and point, that is the one who lifted the {A_WILLOW}; the whole snagged half of the fleet is back in the running because of one heave, and {COMPANION}, unwatched all this while, has kept herself honest in mid-pack
beats.a_fleet_hero_end: ninth over the line, nowhere near the ribbons, and the {FINISH} does not seem to have noticed: six families whose boats came home owe the {A_WILLOW} curtain to {HERO}, and the miller pins the green River Friend badge on her collar with flour still on his thumbs; {A_MILLER} gets his story at the mill door, told twice
beats.a_spare_net: {A_MILLER}'s {A2_BRIDGE_SPARE} is half the length and light as a spoon; {HERO} takes it and flies, and the trade feels fair right up until the {A1_WHEEL_ZONE}, where {COMPANION} has wandered into the churning inner ring and is going around like a leaf on a clock
beats.a_parapet_lunge: kneeling at the parapet rail, {HERO} reaches the stubby net down toward the ring; the rim is a hand too far; three passes, three misses, each one closer than the last, the little net trembling at full stretch above the turning water
beats.a_short_net_end: an inch short is an inch short all afternoon; a marshal's long hook finally lifts {COMPANION} out, dizzy but dry, after the leaders are home; tenth place, one borrowed net, and a plan already growing on the walk home: next year, a pole with an extra arm's reach, and maybe the {A_WILLOW} story will be worth two nets
beats.b_bend: the {ROUTE_B_NAME} is another world: {ROUTE_B_CHAR}, dragonflies stitching the air, cows watching over the fence like judges; only a handful of racers turned this way, and an old-timer on a stump winks as {HERO} passes: the bend rewards the patient, always has
beats.b_heron: at the {B1_HERON} a grey heron stands statue-still exactly where the little fleet must drift past, and the bank path forks: the low cattle track hugs the water, close to {COMPANION} but right past the heron's yellow eye; the flowered top path climbs the rise, loses the boats from sight, and comes down ahead at {B2_POOL} before the fleet can arrive
beats.b_creep: heel and toe past the heron, so close its eye turns like a slow clock; the boats drift by in a hushed parade and the heron, deciding they are neither fish nor frog, lets them; then from the sandy bank downstream comes a sound that is all wrong: a kingfisher, piping alarm over and over
beats.b_alarm: last night's rain has slumped the sandy bank, and the slump is slowly burying a {B1_STONES_NEST} while two kingfishers whir in frantic circles; every boat wake slaps another handful of sand loose; {COMPANION} drifts on with the parade, needing nothing, and the nest needs everything; {B1_WARDEN} checks racers at the plank bridge, minutes ahead for a fast sprinter
beats.b_stones: flat stones from the cattle track laid like a tiny sea wall, handfuls of packed sand, a driftwood plank angled to soften the wakes; it is slow, kneeling, careful work, and the race gurgles away downstream without her while the kingfishers watch from a bare branch, still as two blue thoughts
beats.b_blue_flash: sand cakes {HERO}'s knees from hem to boot; far downstream a cheer says the race is still happening to other people, and here the piping has stopped: one kingfisher tilts, drops, and slips into the {B1_STONES_NEST} mouth above the little stone sea wall, a blue spark going home
beats.b_ivy: {B1_WARDEN} arrives at a run with a spade over her shoulder, sent by somebody's shout; she reads the stones, the plank, the sandy knees, and whistles: nest saved, she says, and I have exactly one junior warden armband in this bag; or, and she points her spade at the field path, sprint that way and you will still see your boat finish
beats.b_rope: they work the nesting bank together, stakes and soft rope and a hand-lettered sign, quiet please, kingfishers at home; {B1_WARDEN} talks about bank swallows and last year's flood like the river is a neighbor she has known forever, and hands {HERO} the armband to knot on herself
beats.b_warden_end: at dusk {FRIEND} appears on the towpath carrying {COMPANION}, netted home safe at the finish by half a dozen borrowed hands; she came in somewhere in the middle, nobody quite counted; on {HERO}'s sleeve is {TOWN}'s first junior river warden armband, and in the sandy bank behind her, safe under stone and driftwood, a blue flash settles for the night
beats.b_field: over the stile and flat out across the buttercups, the race noise rising ahead like a kettle coming to the boil; {HERO} arrives at the rope line with burrs on both socks just as the meadow fleet comes wobbling up the last straight
beats.b_see_finish_end: {COMPANION} bobs over the line eleventh, unhelped and unbothered, and {HERO} cheers her home so loudly that strangers laugh; no ribbon, no armband, and nobody at the finish knows about the little stone sea wall upstream; the kingfishers know; some scores only the river keeps
beats.b_sprint_ivy: {HERO} pelts back along the drifting parade to the plank bridge, where {B1_WARDEN} leans over the rail freeing a snagged boat; the words tumble out in one breath, slumped bank, buried {B1_STONES_NEST}, kingfishers circling; {B1_WARDEN} is moving before the sentence ends, spade kit swinging on her shoulder
beats.b_ivy_runs: {B1_WARDEN} runs like a warden, straight and fast, and pulls ahead and away; {HERO} follows with burning lungs, and at the shallows fork the day splits in two: upstream, the nest bank where {B1_WARDEN}'s spade is already flashing; downstream, the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS}, where the unwatched fleet has begun to ground on the shelf, boat after boat
beats.b_back_track: two jobs, one runner: {B1_WARDEN} could use a second pair of hands passing stones at the nest bank, and nobody at all is minding the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS}, where grounded boats are stacking up on the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS} like plates at a sink; {COMPANION} herself is three boats from grounding
beats.b_nest_help: stone, sand, plank, pass; with four hands the little sea wall grows twice as fast, and {B1_WARDEN} talks the whole time, this stone here, that angle there, in the voice people use when they are quietly deciding something about you
beats.b_brace: the bank holds, the piping stops, and a blue spark slips home into the tunnel; {B1_WARDEN} sits back on her heels, looks at {HERO} properly for the first time, and writes something in the green warden book with a stub of pencil, saying it out loud as she writes: assisted, capably
beats.b_two_hands_end: the warden book closes on {HERO}'s name; down at the merge, {B2_RIVAL2} nets {COMPANION} off the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS} in passing, and she finishes mid-fleet without her skipper; no ribbon travels upstream, but {B1_WARDEN} says the river remembers a wall longer than a race remembers a ribbon, and {B1_WARDEN} would know
beats.b_narrows: {HERO} plants herself at the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS} like a gatekeeper; the first grounded boat lifts free with one practiced flick of the net, then a second, then a fifth, each one waddling off down the deep channel; the parade funnels past her in a wobbling, grateful line
beats.b_shelf_watch: then two at once: {COMPANION} grounds gently an arm's reach away at the near end of the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS}, while at the far end the {TWINS}' shared boat swings broadside to the current and starts to tip its cargo of lucky pebbles; a runner can free one first, not both, and the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS} is longer than it looks
beats.b_twins_first: the long way down the shelf at a dead run; the {TWINS}' boat rights itself at the first touch of the net, pebbles and all, and waddles free; back up the shelf, and {COMPANION} has waited exactly where the sand held her, patient as the bend itself
beats.b_all_free: one flick and {COMPANION} is away, last through the narrows and losing nothing by it; the meadow fleet sails on to the merge strangely whole, not one boat left behind on the shelf, and word of the net at the narrows travels up the line faster than the boats do
beats.b_ferry_end: {COMPANION} finishes fifth, best of the bend fleet, and the old-timer from the stump is at the rope line to see it: told you the bend rewards the patient; the {TWINS} present {HERO} with one lucky pebble each, warm from their pockets, which is two more prizes than the {AWARD_TABLE} gave anyone else
beats.b_acorn_first: nearest first: {COMPANION} lifts free in a blink and rides away down the channel; but the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS} is longer than it looks, and by the time {HERO} reaches the far end the {TWINS}' boat has rolled, spilled its lucky pebbles, and wedged hard under the {B1_SPRINT_NARROWS} lip, needing both hands and long, careful minutes
beats.b_two_at_once_end: everything comes home safe and nothing comes home the way it should: the {TWINS}' boat freed at last but out of the race, {COMPANION} unwatched at the merge, nosed into the reeds and netted free by {B2_RIVAL2}, finishing tenth; on the walk home {HERO} works the problem like a knot: nearest is not always first; some boats need you sooner than yours does
beats.b_top_path: the top path climbs through flowering grass until the whole bend lies below like a painted map: the drifting parade, the statue heron, and ahead, round and green as a coin, {B2_POOL}, where every boat in the bend must pass and every boat slows to a crawl
beats.b_pool_view: {HERO} comes down the rise well ahead of the fleet; the pool wears its duckweed like a bowling green, unbroken edge to edge; on the near bank {B2_RIVAL2} is already pacing and groaning about his own boat {B2_RIVAL2_BOAT}, still upstream: her {B2_RIVAL2_MECH} will clog solid in a boat-length of this stuff
beats.b_pool: {B2_RIVAL2} lays out the problem like a general: the old {B2_CLEAR_CHANNEL} along the near edge runs straight through, but it is choked, and clearing it before the fleet arrives would take two net poles raking together; or, across the railed plank walk, the wardens' {B2_WEED_BOOM} hangs on its hinge at the far lip, and swung open it would widen the one thread of clear current; one runner cannot do both in time
beats.b_clear: two poles raking in rhythm like haymakers, green windrows piling on the bank; a channel opens one boat wide, dark and clean, and {B2_RIVAL2} presses a knob of beeswax into {HERO}'s hand, wax the hull and weed will not grip, family secret; then the first bows of the fleet come nosing into the pool
beats.b_lane_open: the parade funnels into the cleared lane nose to tail, a convoy in single file; from the bank it looks like a green zipper closing behind each hull; then {B2_RIVAL2} goes quiet: at the back, {B2_RIVAL2_BOAT} has arrived, {B2_RIVAL2_MECH} churning, gaining on the slow line with nowhere to pass
beats.b_convoy: {B2_RIVAL2_BOAT} will catch the convoy mid-pool, and a boat that big shoving down a one-boat lane means little boats squeezed out into the weed; there is time to rake a passing loop, a bulge in the middle of the lane where a big hull could swing out and by, but it is more haymaking on arms already tired; or the convoy can take its chances with a bump and a shove
beats.b_passing_loop: sore arms, second wind; the bulge opens in the lane like a wide place in a country road just as {B2_RIVAL2_BOAT} arrives; she swings out, thunders past the little ones with a hand's width to spare, and swings back in, and the convoy never even wobbles
beats.b_pour_out: the pool empties in perfect order, big boat first, little boats after, every hull striped with weed and none the worse; {COMPANION} rides out mid-pack onto the merge current, and {B2_RIVAL2} is shouting about passing loops like he has just invented roads
beats.b_lane_end: {COMPANION} catches the merge current and finishes sixth; {B2_RIVAL2} finds {HERO} at the {AWARD_TABLE} and splits his {TREAT} into two exact halves, engineer fashion; the green lane will be gone by morning, raked weed and all, but every boat in the bend came through it, and both of them know whose arms did that
beats.b_jog_cheer: the bump comes mid-pool: {B2_RIVAL2_BOAT} noses the last boat in line, the shove travels up the convoy like a rumor, and two little boats squeeze out of the lane into the weed field, stuck fast beyond any net's reach; the rest jostle through; {B2_RIVAL2} cannot watch
beats.b_jam_end: the lane jams, unjams, crawls; {COMPANION} wallows out of the pool long after the leaders and finishes out of the ribbons, while the two weed-bound boats wait, safe and cross, for the marshals' punt to gather them at race end; passing loop next year, says {B2_RIVAL2}, and they shake on it like engineers who have just watched a bridge wobble
beats.b_plank_walk: the plank walk drums underfoot, pool sliding green below the handrails; at the far lip the {B2_WEED_BOOM} hangs on its crusted hinge, a floating log gate the wardens swing to corral the weed, its keeper chain held fast by one {B2_BOOM_PIN}
beats.b_boom: one good heave will swing the {B2_WEED_BOOM} partway on its crusted hinge, widening the thread of current to a ribbon before the fleet arrives; or the {B2_BOOM_PIN} could be worked loose first for the full wide swing, minutes of knuckle work while the first bows are already nosing into the weed
beats.b_partway: the hinge shrieks and gives half its arc; the thread of current widens to a bright ribbon down the middle of the green, and the first boats find it like ducks finding a slipway, stringing through the pool in a neat silver line
beats.b_half_gate: half a gate turns out to be most of a race: the ribbon of current carries boat after boat across with barely a wallow, and {COMPANION} takes it neatly, second of the bend fleet, leaf sail drawing; a few heavy boats still shoulder into the weed at the edges and grumble their way across
beats.b_thread_end: {COMPANION} rides the silver thread out of the pool, holds her place down the merge, and finishes seventh; {B1_WARDEN}, passing the boom on her rounds, eyes the half-swung gate, taps her nose, and leaves it exactly where {HERO} heaved it: race day rules, she says, the river likes a thread it can find
beats.b_pin: rust fights back; knuckles sting, the {B2_BOOM_PIN} turns a quarter, sticks, turns again, while behind her the first boats nose into the green and slow like toys winding down; then the {B2_BOOM_PIN} drops into {HERO}'s palm and the keeper chain runs free through its eye
beats.b_full_gate: the {B2_WEED_BOOM} swings wide like a farm gate at milking time, and the pool changes its mind all at once: the thread fattens to a proper current, the green field folds back on both sides, and every boat in the pool, even the early ones stuck fast, pulls free and gathers way
beats.b_floodgate_end: the bend fleet crosses the pool together and arrives at the merge as a surprise armada, seventeen boats where the {ROUTE_A_NAME} crowd expected three; {COMPANION} finishes eighth in the middle of the parade, and the {TWINS}' boat is home because of the {B2_BOOM_PIN} in {HERO}'s pocket, which she keeps, rust and all, on the windowsill all winter
beats.c_cut: the {ROUTE_C_NAME} is the quiet way: {ROUTE_C_CHAR} where footsteps echo and the race noise falls away behind; hardly anyone chose it; ahead, unhurried and enormous, comes the clank, clank of the great {C_GATES}, and a brass bell rings twice
beats.c_gates: the {C_LOCK} itself: two black {C_GATES} tall as barn doors, a waiting pool above them collecting the little fleet like ducklings in a basin, and up on the beam, winding a {C_WINDLASS} with both hands, the {C_LOCK} keeper: grey plait, patched blue coat, eyes bright as bell brass; she marks {HERO}'s arrival with one nod
beats.c_maren: boats for the flight, says {C_KEEPER} the {C_LOCK} keeper, then choose, racer: my {C_LOCK}s, down through both chambers the slow and proper way, and I will tell you how a {C_LOCK} talks while the water works; or the {C2_BYWASH} yonder, the sluice that spills around the {C_LOCK}, quick and rowdy and no promises; the little fleet noses at the {C_GATES}, waiting on the choice
beats.c_pen: the {C_GATES} swing, the fleet pens into the upper {C_CHAMBER}, and the {C_GATES} boom shut behind them like a held breath; hold the bell rope, says {C_KEEPER}, and ring when I say; paddles lift somewhere under the water and the whole {C_CHAMBER} begins, gently, to sink, boats riding down as if the river were a lift
beats.c_story: between clanks, {C_KEEPER} talks: fifty years ago she was a racer with a boat called {C_KEEPER_BOAT}, and on race day the {DEADLINE} rang while she stood at these {C_GATES} waiting for a keeper who never came; she stayed, learned the locks, and keeps them still; {C_KEEPER_BOAT} sits in the {C_LOCK} house window to this day, varnish dusty, race number seven still painted on her bow, a boat that never finished
beats.c_firefly: the lower {C_GATES} open on the last stretch of river, and through the {C_LOCK} house window little {C_KEEPER_BOAT} looks out at the water like a dog at a gate; {C_KEEPER} follows {HERO}'s eyes and says nothing at all, which is a kind of question; the second {C_CHAMBER} still needs working, and the {C_WINDLASS} handles are worn to the shape of fifty years of one pair of hands
beats.c_dust: {C_KEEPER} goes very still, then laughs at the ceiling, fetches {C_KEEPER_BOAT} from the window, and wipes fifty years off the varnish with one sleeve; race number seven, she says, never scratched off; the two boats slide out of the {C_CHAMBER} together, the old one and the young one, and the current takes them side by side
beats.c_hats: down the lower cut, old racers on the bank recognize {C_KEEPER_BOAT} one at a time, and one at a time their hats come off; word runs ahead of the boats; then at the last footbridge a marshal leans over: entered boats only past this point, and number seven is fifty years off the list; the crowd goes quiet; {COMPANION} carries the only race number between them
beats.c_number_swap: {HERO} unpins her race number from {COMPANION}'s mast and hands it up to the marshal, who reads her face, then ties it to {C_KEEPER_BOAT}'s bow just below the painted seven; {COMPANION} sails on unnumbered, flying escort, and the bank noise starts low and builds like weather coming
beats.c_firefly_end: {C_KEEPER_BOAT} crosses the line official at last, fifty years late and not one second early, to the biggest cheer of the day; from the gate beam far upstream, {C_KEEPER} hears it and knows; {COMPANION} slips over after, unofficial and gloriously unbothered, and at the {C_LOCK} house that evening there is {TREAT}, the whole story told properly, and a promise: next year, racer, we launch her again
beats.c_escort: sail on, then, both of them: {C_KEEPER_BOAT} with her painted seven, {COMPANION} flying escort a half-length behind like she was trained to it; the marshal jogs alongside arguing with his own rulebook, and the hats keep coming off all the way down the cut
beats.c_escort_end: at the line the judges rule it Solomon-fashion: {COMPANION} places sixth, and {C_KEEPER_BOAT} enters the record book in fresh ink as escort, with honors, the first boat in {TOWN} history to finish without entering; the book is carried up to the {C_LOCK} for {C_KEEPER} to see, and she taps the entry twice, which from {C_KEEPER} is a parade
beats.c_windlass: {C_KEEPER} sets {HERO}'s hands on the worn handles and stands behind her like a spar: a {C_LOCK} talks in clanks and hushes, listen for the hush; the paddles answer, the {C_CHAMBER} drains, and the great {C_GATES} swing at the push of one girl leaning her whole weight, which is exactly how it should feel, says {C_KEEPER}, like the river agreeing with you
beats.c_stragglers: from the gate top {HERO} spots three straggler boats arriving in the upper pound, little kids' boats, late and slow, and the {DEADLINE} not far off; {C_KEEPER} is oiling the far gate; a flight takes minutes to work, and there are only so many minutes left: a first solo lockage would send the stragglers down in time, and cost {COMPANION} her own run; {C_KEEPER} catches the look and shrugs: your {C_GATES}, keeper
beats.c_solo_flight: clank, hush, fall: three little boats ride {HERO}'s first solo lockage down the flight, their owners watching through the gate rails like it is magic done slowly; she works the second {C_CHAMBER} alone, the {C_GATES} boom at her word, hers, and the stragglers sail out onto the last stretch with the bell still unrung
beats.c_brass_key: {C_KEEPER} watches the last gate settle, then unhooks something from her belt that has hung there so long it has worn a pale stripe in the leather: a brass gate key on a red cord; she does not make a speech about it; she just holds it out on one weathered palm
beats.c_keeper_end: {COMPANION} misses the bell by minutes and nobody in the {C_LOCK} house minds; junior keeper of the flight, says {C_KEEPER}, first one in fifty years, and the key sits warm on its red cord while three little kids' boats dry on the sill beside {C_KEEPER_BOAT}; some races, {HERO} understands now, are won by staying, and the {C_LOCK} will talk to her all summer
beats.c_bell_run: {C_KEEPER} takes back the {C_WINDLASS} with a nod, go on then, I will lock the little ones down myself; {HERO} takes the towpath at a dead sprint, and behind her the flight begins to clank through its work in old, quick, certain hands
beats.c_surge: the worked flight sends its long smooth push of water down the last stretch, and {COMPANION} rides the {C_LOCK} surge past one tired boat after another; ahead the bell tower flag is still flying, and the noise of the merge crowd rises to meet them
beats.c_bell_end: {COMPANION} slides over the line as the {DEADLINE} begins to swing: sixth place, and the only {C_LOCK} route boat home inside the count; under the bell's ringing, if you know what to listen for, come two far-off clanks from the flight, which is {C_LOCK} for well run, racer; the little kids' boats come down safe after, exactly as promised
beats.c_bywash: the {C2_BYWASH} then, quick and rowdy: a mossy stone sluice that spills around the {C_LOCK} in a long white {A1_WALL_CHUTE}; the little fleet tips in like minnows down a drainpipe, and {HERO} runs the mown path along the top of the sluice wall, hedge on one side, flying spray rattling off the stones below on the other
beats.c_wall_path: the sluice braids and pleats below the wall, and {COMPANION} bucks through it upright, mostly; ahead, halfway down, a {C2_PORTAGE_POST} stands at the path fork where racers may lift their own boats out and carry across the {C_LOCK} island; beyond it, faint, comes the roar of the {C2_FAN} where the {C2_BYWASH} spends itself
beats.c_white_post: the {C2_PORTAGE_POST}, rule two made of wood: {HERO} may scoop {COMPANION} out here, her own boat and no other, and portage the mown path over the {C_LOCK} island to relaunch in calm water below; or leave her running the whole rowdy {C2_BYWASH} and sprint the wall path to meet the fleet at the {C2_FAN}, where the standing waves flip somebody every single year
beats.c_rapids: the last pitch of the sluice is all white: {COMPANION} vanishes in spray, pops up, vanishes, pops up like a cork with opinions; behind her two smaller boats take the big wave wrong and flip, washing hull-up onto the {C2_GRATING} at the {C2_FAN}'s edge, and up the path come the {TWINS}, running and crying at once
beats.c_grating: the {TWINS}' boats sit hull-up on the {C2_GRATING}, a stride past any net's reach from the path, but the {C2_GRATING_PIN} that swings the {C2_GRATING} is up on the plank bridge, an easy pull for someone tall enough who knows it is there; {COMPANION} meanwhile is through and away, riding the {C2_FAN}'s main tongue toward the merge with nobody watching; the {TWINS} hiccup and point and pull at {HERO}'s sleeve
beats.c_pin_pull: up the plank bridge, find the {C2_GRATING_PIN}, pull: the {C2_GRATING} tips with a rusty sigh and both little hulls roll right side up and slide free into the calm below, where two lifts of the net set them on the bank, dripping and whole; the {TWINS} hug an elbow each like limpets
beats.c_twin_parade: relaunched in the flat water below the fan, the {TWINS}' boats waddle off toward the merge; {HERO} jogs the last field with a twin holding each hand, their boats paddling ahead, while {COMPANION} is long out of sight, sailing her own race somewhere past the merge
beats.c_twins_end: word comes back up the path that {COMPANION} crossed the line ninth all by herself and was lifted out safe by the finish marshal; the {TWINS} tell the pin story at the {AWARD_TABLE} twice each, with actions; and for the rest of the summer two small crews follow {HERO} up and down the towpath calling her captain
beats.c_shout: the {C2_FAN}'s roar eats half the words, but the older twin catches the ones that matter, {C2_GRATING_PIN}, BRIDGE, TIP, and the two of them scramble for the plank bridge together; {HERO} pelts on down the wall path after her own vanishing sail, guilt and hurry taking one leg each
beats.c_merge_race: {COMPANION} rejoins the main river in a knot of {ROUTE_A_NAME} boats and holds her own down the merge, leaf sail pulling hard; behind and far away comes a double splash and then a high double cheer, twin-sized, which loosens something in {HERO}'s chest just in time for the last bend
beats.c_fifth_end: fifth over the line, best of the {C2_BYWASH} boats, and a fine loud finish; at the {AWARD_TABLE} the {TWINS} arrive towing both rescued boats and tell the pin story with {HERO} in it anyway, more heroic than she remembers being; she is glad, and a little sorry she only heard the splash, and both of those feelings keep
beats.c_island: {COMPANION} comes out at the white post dripping under {HERO}'s arm, and the island path is another country: bee-loud plum shade, and in the middle of it a picnic blanket of long-way-rounders, three racers trading gear like market day, wax, spare sail cloth, lucky pebbles; {C2_ISLAND_TRADER} looks up from her needle and thread and points it at {COMPANION}'s fraying leaf sail: wind is rising on the last reach, trade you a stitch-up
beats.c_blanket: {C2_ISLAND_TRADER}'s price is the {TREAT} in {HERO}'s pocket: cake for a quick sail surgery and a strip of her {C2_ISLAND_ITEM} to stiffen the leaf's spine; a fair trade, but the stone step relaunch is just down the path, and every breath spent trading is a boat-length given away to the {C2_BYWASH} fleet roaring past beyond the trees
beats.c_stitch: the needle flashes, the frayed leaf gets a dragonfly-blue spine, and the sail comes back stiff and bright; {HERO} breaks the {TREAT} and shares it around the blanket anyway, which makes {C2_ISLAND_TRADER} look at her the way {B1_WARDEN} looks at rivers; overhead, the plum leaves have started to stir
beats.c_wind: relaunched off the stone step at the island's foot, {COMPANION} meets the promised wind broadside on the last reach; the mended sail fills without a wrinkle and she skips across the water like a flat stone thrown well, past one wallowing {C2_BYWASH} boat after another
beats.c_sail_end: {COMPANION} carves through the merge crowd and takes second place on the last reach, the dragonfly stitch pulling the whole way; {C2_ISLAND_TRADER} arrives whooping with the blanket still around her shoulders like a cape: some wins are stitched, not sailed, she says, and refuses her half of the ribbon but accepts her half of the glory
beats.c_stone_step: no trade: down to the stone step and back in the water before any other portager, old frayed sail and all; for three glorious bends {COMPANION} leads the portage pack, while the wind {C2_ISLAND_TRADER} promised begins to comb the water dark from the far bank
beats.c_lead_bends: the wind arrives in earnest on the last reach and every sail on the river fills at once; {COMPANION} heels, surges, and for one breathless moment gains on the leaders; then the frayed leaf gives at the seam with a sound like paper tearing, small but final
beats.c_torn_end: {COMPANION} wallows home under half a sail, just past the bell, logged home safe with no place; at the {AWARD_TABLE} {C2_ISLAND_TRADER} finds {HERO} and presses the strip of {C2_ISLAND_ITEM} into her hand, no trade, keep it for next year; the torn leaf comes down off the mast that night, folded like a letter {HERO} means to answer

a_neck.ch_a_neck_1: Run to the fork at the {A_ISLAND}.

a_wheel.ch_a_wheel_1: Take the wheel side, under the {A1_WHEEL_ARCH}.

a_swirl.ch_a_swirl_2: Wait, poised, for {COMPANION}'s ring to come round.

a_wait_ring.ch_a_wait_ring_1: Race the freed {COMPANION} down the outflow.

a_wall.ch_a_wall_1: Run up to the blocked {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth.

a_shoulder_offer.ch_a_shoulder_offer_1: Stay and keep the {A1_WALL_CHUTE} mouth clear.

a_shoulder_ride.ch_a_shoulder_ride_1: Scramble down and run for the {A1_WALL_CHUTE}.

a_crew_ask.ch_a_crew_ask_2: Point at the seam and fix {FRIEND_BOAT} first.

b_ivy.ch_b_ivy_1: Stay and rope off the nest bank with {B1_WARDEN}.

b_shelf_watch.ch_b_shelf_watch_2: Free your {COMPANION} first, she is nearest.

b_pool.ch_b_pool_1: Team up and rake the {B2_CLEAR_CHANNEL} clear.

b_boom.ch_b_boom_2: Work the {B2_BOOM_PIN} loose for the full swing.

c_firefly.ch_c_firefly_2: Ask her to teach you the {C_WINDLASS}.

c_stragglers.ch_c_stragglers_2: Hand back the {C_WINDLASS} and run for the bell.

c_bell_run.ch_c_bell_run_1: Race the {C_LOCK} water down the last stretch.

c_white_post.ch_c_white_post_1: Let her run the {C2_BYWASH} to the fan.

c_rapids.ch_c_rapids_1: Meet the {TWINS} at the gravel fan.

c_grating.ch_c_grating_1: Take the bridge and pull the {C2_GRATING_PIN}.
c_grating.ch_c_grating_2: Shout the {TWINS} the pin trick and run on.

c_pin_pull.ch_c_pin_pull_1: Relaunch the {TWINS}' boats in the calm.
